title: Intensely Fatty Filipino Haricot Beans Pinakbet
description: A rich and savory Filipino dish featuring haricot beans in a flaky pastry crust, perfect for a special occasion or date night. This pinakbet variation is both decadent and low-fat-friendly by using lean protein and balanced ingredients.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat oil over medium heat and saute the onion and tomato until softened.
Add the bagoong, salt, and pepper, and stir to combine.
Stir in the mashed haricot beans and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Roll out the pastry dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Spoon the haricot bean mixture onto one half of the pastry, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Top with crisped pork belly and brush the edges of the pastry with the beaten egg.
Fold the other half of the pastry over the filling and press the edges to seal.
Brush the top of the pastry with the remaining egg and cut a few slits in the top to allow steam to escape.
Bake the pinakbet for 25-30 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown.
